notes: IBM retired HCL Notes branding; HCL acquired Notes/Domino from IBM 2019; HCL Notes 14 released 2024 but market share negligible
Web-enable Notes databases via browser; continues under HCL Domino; niche legacy installed base
Interliant co-developed Lotus Instant!Host strategy; dissolved with Interliant bankruptcy
Packet-switched WAN protocol; largely replaced by Frame Relay then IP VPN; essentially obsolete
WAN protocol used by Interliant; replaced by MPLS and broadband internet
Dial-up Internet access protocol; TCP/IP remains foundational; PPP largely replaced by broadband
Early wireless data networks; ARDIS became Motient; both replaced by cellular data (GPRS/3G/4G)
Dominant delivery mechanism; Domino's web browser access feature was ahead of its time in 1997
